codeReview.summary: The implementation cleanly resolves the feature's reported inconsistencies: the evidence surface now uses the minted Base-contract supply, modeled social-vault gating is explicitly framed as planned policy instead of an in-progress feature state, the whale/pro flywheel node no longer overstates maturity, and the graph-shell overview badges now better match the dominant maturity of their underlying surfaces. I did not find blocking or non-blocking defects specific to this feature in the reviewed diff.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Flywheel status data still lives inline inside the component rather than in a shared typed content module, so consistency checks have to render UI just to inspect core architecture content. The shared-state docs prefer typed local content/data modules for reusable surface models, but they do not currently call out the flywheel as one of those models.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/components/architecture/FlywheelExplorer.tsx:24-155 defines FLYWHEEL_NODES inline; src/components/architecture/__tests__/flywheel-status-consistency.test.tsx:7-8 explicitly notes the test must render the component because the data is inline; .factory/library/content-model.md and .factory/library/architecture.md both describe shared typed content-model expectations for major site surfaces.

summary: Pass. The commit fixes the stale evidence-surface supply example, reconciles the modeled social-vault and whale-revenue maturity framing, and aligns the graph-shell dominant-status badges with the current surface mix. The only follow-up worth capturing is a shared-state convention gap: flywheel content remains inline instead of living in the typed content layer.
